creator: yokota@zodiac.mech.utsunomiya-u.ac.jp
text:
>>Number:         30200
>>Category:       kern
>>Synopsis:       Bug in psm in 4.4-RC
>>Confidential:   no
>>Severity:       non-critical
>>Priority:       low
>>Responsible:    freebsd-bugs
>>State:          open
[...]
>>Environment:
>FreeBSD chris.gintera.net 4.4-RC FreeBSD 4.4-RC #9: Wed Aug 29 12:33:18 MDT 20
>01     chris@chris.gintera.net:/usr/src/sys/compile/CHEROKEE  i386
>>Description:
>After upgrading from a 2 month-old 4.3-STABLE install to 4.4-RC, the scrollwhe
>el on my logitech optical mouse would invoke button-3 when scrolling up in the
> root window, and when changing scroll direction in galeon.
>>How-To-Repeat:
>Roll the scrollwheel up and down where ever button-3 would create a menu.
>>Fix:
>Reverting rev 1.23.2.4 of src/sys/isa/psm.c fixes the problem

Ok, I will see to this.

Would you please supply some more information by doing the following?

1. Use psm.c rev 1.23.2.5, add
options PSM_DEBUG=2
in your kernel config, and rebuild the kernel.
2. Start the system by giving 'boot -v' at the loader prompt.
3. Send me output from dmesg.
4. Tell me the exact name and the model number (printed on the belly)
   of your Logitec mouse.

Thank you for your cooperation.

Kazu
